content.problemStatement:
Finally, a COVID vaccine is out on the market and the Chefland government has asked you to form a plan to distribute it to the public as soon as possible. There are a total of NNN people with ages a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.

There is only one hospital where vaccination is done and it is only possible to vaccinate up to DDD people per day. Anyone whose age is ‚Č•80\ge 80‚Č•80 or ‚Č§9\le 9‚Č§9 is considered to be at risk. On each day, you may not vaccinate both a person who is at risk and a person who is not at risk. Find the smallest number of days needed to vaccinate everyone.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and DDD.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the smallest required number of days.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We do not need to worry about how the people are grouped, since only one person can be vaccinated in a single day. We require as many days as there are people.
Example case 2: There are two people at risk and three people who are not at risk. One optimal strategy is to vaccinate the two people at risk on day 111 and the remaining three on the next 222 days.
